title: Methods and apparatuses for managing external approval provisioning and external messaging communication requests in a group-based communication system
abstract: Various embodiments of the present disclosure are directed to a group-based communication system that is configured to more efficiently manage resource sharing requests. In particular, in some embodiments, the group-based communication system is configured to enable admin users to use an external service, such as an external approval provisioning service, that is separate and distinct from the group-based communication system to manage group-based communication resource sharing requests. In other embodiments, the group- based communication system is configured to allow communication with external users and to trigger new user onboarding protocols through the ingestion of external user directories associated with a sponsor user.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS We Claim: 1. One or more non-transitory computer-readable media storing computer- executable instructions that, when executed by a processor, perform a method of transmitting a group-based communication system message to a target external user, the method comprising: presenting, to a sending user via a user interface of a group-based communication system client on a sending user client device, a recipient entry field programmed to receive internal user identifiers associated with the group-based communication system and external user identifiers; receiving, from the sending user and via the user interface, a target external user identifier associated with the target external user and message data of the group- based communication system message, wherein the target external user identifier is external to the group-based communication system; and causing transmission of the message data to an external messaging resource address associated with the target external user identifier for display by an external messaging resource as a linked message object. 2. The media of claim 1, wherein the method further comprises: further receiving, from the sending user and via the user interface, a target internal user identifier associated with a target internal user, wherein the target internal user is a user of the group-based communication system; and further causing transmission of the message data to the target internal user via the group-based communication system for display by the group-based communication system client on a receiving user client device. 3. The media of claim 1, wherein the method further comprises: after receiving a partial entry by the sending user of the target external user identifier and prior to receiving the target external user identifier, providing, to the sending user, at least one suggested external user identifier matching the partial 

 entry and at least one suggested internal user identifier matching the partial entry. 4. The media of claim 3, wherein the at least one suggested external user identifier is based on a previously ingested user contacts list associated with the sending user. 5. The media of claim 1, wherein the target external user identifier is an email address of the target external user. 6. The media of claim 1, wherein the target external user is external to a group- based communication organization of the sending user. 7. The media of claim 1, wherein the target external user is external to a group- based communication system workspace of the sending user. 8. A method of transmitting a group-based communication system message to a target external user, the method comprising: presenting, to a sending user via a user interface of a group-based communication system on a sending user client device, a recipient entry field programmed to receive internal user identifiers associated with the group-based communication system and external user identifiers; receiving, from the sending user and via the user interface, a target external user identifier associated with the target external user and message data of the group- based communication system message, wherein the target external user identifier is external to the group-based communication system; and causing transmission of the message data to an external messaging resource address associated with the target external user identifier for display by an external messaging resource as a linked message object. 9. The method of claim 8, further comprising: further receiving, from the sending user and via the user interface, a target internal user identifier associated with a target internal user, wherein the target internal user is a user of the group-based communication system; and further causing transmission of the message data to the target internal user via the group-based communication system for display by the group-based communication system client on a receiving user client device. 10. The method of claim 8, further comprising: after receiving a partial entry by the sending user of the target external user identifier and prior to receiving the target external user identifier, providing, to the sending user, at least one suggested external user identifier matching the partial entry and at least one suggested internal user identifier matching the partial entry. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the at least one suggested external user identifier is based on a previously ingested user contacts list associated with the sending user. 12. The method of claim 8, wherein the target external user identifier is an email address of the target external user. 13. The method of claim 8, wherein the target external user is external to a group- based communication system organization of the sending user. 14. The method of claim 8, wherein the target external user is external to a group- based communication system workspace of the sending user. 15. A system for transmitting a group-based communication system message to a target external user, comprising: a group-based communication system server; and a sending user client device programmed to: present, to a sending user via a user interface of a group-based communication system client, a recipient entry field programmed to receive internal user identifiers associated with the group-based communication system and external user identifiers; receive, from the sending user and via the user interface, a target external user identifier associated with the target external user and message data of the group-based communication system message, wherein the target external user identifier is external to the group-based communication system; and cause transmission of the message data, via the group-based communication system server, to an external messaging resource address associated with the target external user identifier for display by an external messaging resource as a linked message object. 16. The system of claim 15, further comprising: a receiving user client device; and wherein the sending user client device is further programmed to: further receive, from the sending user and via the user interface, a target internal user identifier associated with a target internal user, wherein the target internal user is a user of the group-based communication system; and further cause transmission of the message data to the target internal user via the group-based communication system server for display by the group- based communication system client on the receiving user client device. 17. The system of claim 15, wherein the sending user client device is further programmed to: after receiving a partial entry by the sending user of the target external user identifier and prior to receiving the target external user identifier, providing, to the sending user, at least one suggested external user identifier matching the partial 

 entry and at least one suggested internal user identifier matching the partial entry. 18. The system of claim 80, wherein the at least one suggested external user identifier is based on a previously ingested user contacts list associated with the sending user. 19. The system of claim 15, wherein the target external user identifier is an email address of the target external user. 20. The system of claim 15, wherein the target external user is external to one of a group-based communication system organization of the sending user and a group-based communication system organization of the sending user.
